findings.title: Floating Pragma Version
findings.description: The contract uses a floating pragma (^). This allows compilation with multiple compiler versions, which may introduce inconsistencies between deployments.
findings.impact: Different compiler versions may produce different bytecode or include different known bugs.
findings.recommendation: Lock the pragma to a specific version, e.g., pragma solidity 0.8.20;

findings.title: ERC-20 Allowance Race Condition
findings.description: The standard ERC-20 approve() function is vulnerable to a front-running race condition. If a user changes an allowance from N to M, a spender can front-run and spend both N and M.
findings.impact: A spender could extract more tokens than intended during an allowance change.
findings.recommendation: This is a known ERC-20 limitation. Consider adding increaseAllowance/decreaseAllowance helper functions, or instruct users to set allowance to 0 before changing it.

cwe.name: Improper Check or Handling of Exceptional Conditions

findings.title: Fixed Supply (Constructor-Only Mint)
findings.description: Token minting occurs only in the constructor. The supply is fixed at deployment with no public or external mint function available.
findings.impact: No inflation risk. The token supply cannot be increased after deployment.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. This is a secure pattern for fixed-supply tokens.

findings.title: Initial Supply Concentration
findings.location: Constructor — full supply to single address
findings.description: The entire token supply is minted to a single address (custodian/deployer) in the constructor. This is standard for ERC-20 launches but creates concentration risk.
findings.impact: The initial holder controls 100% of supply. If tokens are not distributed, this creates market dump risk and governance centralization.
findings.recommendation: This is an economic/governance consideration, not a smart contract vulnerability. Recommend transparent vesting schedules and distribution plans.

cwe.name: Improper Check or Handling of Exceptional Conditions

architectureOverview: Unknown is implemented as a erc721 on BSC. The contract is compiled with Solidity 0.8.20. It inherits from OpenZeppelin's audited library contracts for standardized functionality. The contract is non-upgradeable with no proxy pattern detected. Ownership has been renounced to the zero address, making the contract immutable and trustless. The token follows a fixed supply model with no external mint capability. The contract scope comprises 361 lines of code.

attackSurface.title: Owner Key Compromise -> Protocol Takeover
attackSurface.description: Ownership has been renounced, eliminating the risk of owner key compromise. No privileged functions remain accessible to any external account. The contract is effectively trustless.

attackSurface.title: Supply Manipulation -> Fixed Supply Verification
attackSurface.description: The contract implements a fixed supply model. No mint function is present in the bytecode — tokens were allocated at deployment and can only be transferred, not created. This architecture eliminates the supply inflation attack vector entirely. Total supply is immutable and verifiable on-chain.
attackSurface.narrativeImpact: No supply inflation risk — fixed or deflationary supply model
attackSurface.mitigation: No mint function detected — supply fixed at deployment. No mitigation needed.
attackSurface.simulationResult: No mint() function found in contract ABI or bytecode. Supply is fixed at totalSupply() — verified immutable.

objectives: Identify security vulnerabilities in the deployed smart contract
Evaluate standard compliance and edge cases

riskModel.disclaimer: Severity assignments are based on the auditor's professional judgment at the time of review.

auditorProfile.description: Independent smart contract security firm specializing in manual adversarial review combined with AI-guided static analysis.
auditorProfile.methodology: Manual adversarial review + automated static analysis (Slither, Mythril) + AI-assisted pattern detection + on-chain bytecode verification.

statements: This audit was conducted between 2026-04-11 and 2026-04-21 as a time-boxed security review.
The scope is limited to the smart contract source code listed in the Scope Table above. Off-chain components, frontend code, backend APIs, and deployment infrastructure were NOT reviewed.
This review does not constitute a formal mathematical verification of the contract's correctness. No guarantee is made that all vulnerabilities have been identified.
Findings and severity ratings are based on the auditor's professional judgment at the time of review and may change if the code is modified.
This audit does not constitute financial, legal, or investment advice. It is a technical assessment only.

The security of the deployed contract also depends on factors outside the scope of this audit, including but not limited to: private key management, deployment procedures, and third-party dependencies.
limitations.disclaimer: DISCLAIMER & LIMITATION OF LIABILITY: This security audit report is provided 'as-is' for informational purposes only. Solay39 and its auditors make no representations or warranties, express or implied, regarding the completeness, accuracy, or reliability of this assessment. The auditor accepts no liability for any losses, damages, or claims arising from the use of this report or the audited smart contracts. This report should not be relied upon as a sole indicator of security. Users and stakeholders are advised to conduct their own independent due diligence before interacting with the audited protocol.

finalAssessment.securityPosture: The Unknown contract demonstrates a strong security posture with well-implemented access controls, standard-compliant code, and no critical vulnerabilities identified during review.
finalAssessment.productionReadiness: The contract is considered production-ready pending resolution of all identified findings.
keyRisksToMonitor: External dependency updates and potential upstream vulnerabilities
On-chain monitoring for abnormal transaction patterns

economicAttackVectors.title: Flash Loan Price Manipulation
economicAttackVectors.description: No price-sensitive DeFi logic detected. Flash loan attack surface is minimal.

economicAttackVectors.title: MEV Sandwich Attack
economicAttackVectors.description: No AMM swap logic detected. MEV sandwich attack surface is minimal.
economicAttackVectors.exploitCondition: Mempool visibility + no slippage protection + no deadline parameter
economicAttackVectors.estimatedLoss: 0.1% - 5% per transaction depending on liquidity depth
economicAttackVectors.mitigation: Enforce slippage tolerance (amountOutMin), add deadline parameter, use Flashbots Protect

economicAttackVectors.title: Liquidity Drain via Uncapped Mint
economicAttackVectors.description: No uncapped mint detected. Liquidity drain via inflation is not applicable.
economicAttackVectors.exploitCondition: Compromised minter key + active DEX liquidity pool
economicAttackVectors.mitigation: Supply cap, multisig for minter role, timelock on mint operations
